mercury.example: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun!
mercury.fun_fact: Mercury has no moons and no atmosphere.

venus.example: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system!
venus.fun_fact: Venus spins backwards compared to other planets.

earth.example: Earth is our home! We live on this blue planet.
earth.fun_fact: Earth is the only planet with life that we know of.

mars.example: Mars is called the Red Planet because it looks red!
mars.fun_fact: Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system.

jupiter.example: Jupiter is the biggest planet of all!
jupiter.fun_fact: Jupiter has a giant storm called the Great Red Spot.

saturn.example: Saturn has beautiful rings around it!
saturn.fun_fact: Saturn's rings are made of ice and rock.

uranus.example: Uranus spins on its side like a rolling ball!
uranus.fun_fact: Uranus is sometimes called an 'ice giant'.

neptune.example: Neptune is very blue and very windy!
neptune.fun_fact: Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system.

moon.example: The Moon shines at night and orbits Earth!
moon.fun_fact: The Moon is about 4.5 billion years old.

sun.example: The Sun gives us light and heat every day!
sun.fun_fact: The Sun is a star, not a planet!

pluto.example: Pluto is a dwarf planet far away from the Sun.
pluto.fun_fact: Pluto is smaller than Earth's Moon.

comet.example: Comets have long, shiny tails made of ice and dust!
comet.fun_fact: Comets orbit the Sun just like planets.

asteroid.example: Asteroids are big rocks that float in space.
asteroid.fun_fact: There is a belt of asteroids between Mars and Jupiter.

galaxy.example: Our galaxy is called the Milky Way!
galaxy.fun_fact: There are billions of galaxies in the universe.

star.example: Stars twinkle in the night sky.
star.fun_fact: Some stars are much bigger than our Sun.
